data.content:
ins_camera
**Summary of ins_camera:**  

ins_camera is a namespace or organizational entity primarily associated with camera technology, particularly focusing on resolution standards, settings, and configurations. It contains definitions, enumerations, and specifications related to camera functionality, including resolution modes, exposure settings, and other camera-related parameters. The namespace appears to be part of a software development kit (SDK) or a broader photography technology framework, likely used for defining and managing camera operations in software applications. While descriptions vary slightly—some emphasizing its role as a namespace and others suggesting it may also represent an organization—the consistent theme is its involvement in camera resolution and settings management.  

In summary, ins_camera serves as a structured repository for camera-related technical specifications, whether as part of a software library, SDK, or a broader camera technology initiative. Its primary purpose is to standardize and provide accessible definitions for camera resolutions, exposure modes, and other configurable parameters in digital imaging systems.

Namespace List
A categorized list of namespaces in the documentation, providing organization to code elements.

DoxyList
A documentation generation system that creates structured lists of code elements.

ExposureSettings
Configuration class for manual/automatic camera exposure parameters like ISO and shutter speed.<SEP>ExposureSettings is a class within the ins_camera namespace that handles camera exposure settings.

CaptureSettings
CaptureSettings is a class in the ins_camera namespace that manages temporary camera settings for capture.<SEP>Controls image processing parameters including saturation, white balance and brightness.

RecordParams
RecordParams is a struct within the ins_camera namespace, likely related to video recording parameters.

CameraFunctionMode
CameraFunctionMode is an enum in the ins_camera namespace defining different camera operation modes.

VideoResolution
VideoResolution is an enum in the ins_camera namespace specifying different video resolution options.

include/camera/photography_settings.h
Header file containing camera photography settings definitions and classes

ins_camera::ExposureSettings
Class handling camera exposure parameters and configurations

ins_camera::CaptureSettings
A class or module in the camera software that handles various capture settings like saturation, white balance, brightness, sharpness, and contrast.<SEP>Class managing temporary camera settings for capture operations

ins_camera::RecordParams
Structure containing parameters for video recording operations

ins_camera::CameraFunctionMode
An enum or class defining camera operation modes like normal image and video capture.<SEP>Enumeration defining different operational modes for the camera

ins_camera::VideoResolution
Enumeration specifying available video resolution options

memory
Standard library header for memory management utilities

unordered_map
Standard library header for hash map containers

PhotographyOptions_ExposureMode
An enumeration of different exposure modes for photography settings.

PhotographyOptions_ExposureOptions_Program_AUTO
Automatic exposure mode in photography settings.

PhotographyOptions_ExposureOptions_Program_ISO_PRIORITY
ISO priority exposure mode in photography settings.

PhotographyOptions_ExposureOptions_Program_SHUTTER_PRIORITY
A photography setting category indicating a shutter priority mode in camera exposure options.<SEP>Shutter priority exposure mode in photography settings.

PhotographyOptions_ExposureOptions_Program_MANUAL
A photography setting category indicating a manual mode in camera exposure options.

PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance
A category representing white balance settings in photography, including various temperature presets.

PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance_WB_UNKNOWN
An unknown white balance setting in photography options.

PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance_WB_AUTO
An automatic white balance setting in photography options.

PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance_WB_2700K
A white balance setting at 2700K in photography options.

PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance_WB_4000K
A white balance setting at 4000K in photography options.

PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance_WB_5000K
A white balance setting at 5000K in photography options.

PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance_WB_6500K
A white balance setting at 6500K in photography options.

PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance_WB_7500K
A white balance setting at 7500K in photography options.

PhotographyOptions_ExposureOptions_Program
A category representing different exposure program modes in photography settings, including shutter priority and manual modes.

Insta360
Insta360 is a company known for producing 360-degree cameras and related software, such as their Studio application for viewing panoramic content.<SEP>Insta360 is a company that produces 360-degree cameras and provides software development kits (SDKs) for developers.<SEP>Insta360 is a company that produces 360 cameras, including models like ONE X, ONE X2, X3, and X4.

CameraSDK-Cpp
CameraSDK-Cpp is a C++ library designed to control Insta360 cameras, offering functionalities like camera discovery, preview, and recording.

ONE X
ONE X is a discontinued Insta360 camera model that requires a special firmware upgrade and USB mode settings for SDK control.<SEP>The ONE X is a discontinued 360 camera model from Insta360.

ONE R Twin Edition
ONE R Twin Edition is a discontinued Insta360 camera model that requires specific USB mode settings for SDK control.<SEP>The ONE R Twin Edition is a discontinued 360 camera model from Insta360.

ONE X2
ONE X2 is an Insta360 camera model that requires USB mode settings for SDK control.<SEP>The ONE X2 is a current 360 camera model from Insta360.

ONE RS 1-Inch 360 Edition
ONE RS 1-Inch 360 Edition is a discontinued Insta360 camera model that requires specific USB mode settings for SDK control.<SEP>The ONE RS 1-Inch 360 Edition is a discontinued 360 camera model from Insta360.

X3
The X3 is a current 360 camera model from Insta360.<SEP>X3 is an Insta360 camera model that requires USB mode settings for SDK control.

X4
The X4 is a camera model mentioned in the text, with specific requirements for shooting modes and preview stream resolution.<SEP>X4 is an Insta360 camera model that requires USB mode settings for SDK control.<SEP>The X4 is a current 360 camera model from Insta360.

Windows
An operating system platform where Visual Studio 2015 is recommended for development.<SEP>Windows is a supported platform for the CameraSDK-Cpp, requiring Windows 7 or later (x64).<SEP>Windows is a supported platform for MediaSDK-Cpp, requiring Windows 7 or later (x64 only).

Linux
Linux is a supported platform for MediaSDK-Cpp, primarily tested on Ubuntu.<SEP>Linux is a supported platform for the CameraSDK-Cpp, requiring specific distributions like Ubuntu 18.04, 20.04, or 22.04 (x86_64).

libusb
libusb is a driver required for Linux systems to detect and control Insta360 cameras via the CameraSDK-Cpp.

libusbK
libusbK is a driver required for Windows systems to detect and control Insta360 cameras via the CameraSDK-Cpp.

DeviceDiscovery
SDK class for detecting and listing available Insta360 cameras.<SEP>The DeviceDiscovery class is responsible for detecting and connecting to cameras to retrieve basic information like serial numbers.

USB Mode
Critical camera setting (Android mode) required for SDK communication.

U-Disk Mode
Alternative camera storage mode that must be disabled for SDK use.

zadig
Windows utility for installing libusbK drivers.

apt-get
Linux package manager command for driver installation.

lsusb
Linux command to verify camera detection (vendor ID 0x2e1a).

DeviceDescriptor
DeviceDescriptor contains necessary DeviceConnectionInfo of the camera and is used in the camera connection process.

Camera
The Camera class is part of the SDK and is used to open, close, and interact with the camera for operations like taking pictures and recording.<SEP>The camera is a device that disconnects clients after 10 seconds without receiving a 'state' command and provides various state responses.<SEP>The device executing the start_preview command and processing video/audio streams.

http://localhost:9099
This URL is the endpoint for accessing images and videos on the camera via an HTTP tunnel.

Preview Stream
The live video output generated by the start_preview command.<SEP>Unstitched dual-fisheye video stream in H.264 format, with model-specific resolution requirements.

TakePhoto
Camera operation that captures still images and returns URLs for accessing the photos.

StartRecording
Initiates video capture with configurable parameters like resolution and bitrate.

MediaUrl
Container for multiple origin URLs and low-bitrate variants of captured media.

ins_camera::PhotographyOptions_WhiteBalance_WB_AUTO
A setting within the photography options that automatically adjusts the white balance for optimal image quality.

ins_camera::CameraFunctionMode::FUNCTION_MODE_NORMAL_IMAGE
A function mode in the camera software for capturing normal images with specified settings.

ins_camera::CameraFunctionMode::FUNCTION_MODE_NORMAL_VIDEO
A function mode in the camera software for capturing normal videos with specified settings.

Visual Studio 2015
An integrated development environment (IDE) recommended for use on Windows for compiling and running the camera software.

g++
A compiler recommended for use on Ubuntu for compiling and running the camera software.<SEP>g++ is the GNU C++ compiler, with version 7.5.0 specified for compiling the example code.<SEP>g++ is a compiler for C++ programs, with version 7.5.0 specified for compiling the MediaSDKDemo.

data.content:
X4 HDR photos
A type of photo output that is integrated into the machine, likely referring to high dynamic range photography.

std::make_shared
A C++ utility function used to create a shared pointer for managing the CaptureSettings object.

saturation
An image parameter set to 60 in the CaptureSettings, affecting color intensity.<SEP>Saturation parameter with a range from 0 to 255.

white balance
A photography setting configured to AUTO mode in the CaptureSettings for automatic color temperature adjustment.

brightness
An image parameter set to 30 in the CaptureSettings, controlling light levels.<SEP>Brightness parameter with a range from -255 to 255.

sharpness
An image parameter set to 4 in the CaptureSettings, affecting edge definition.<SEP>Sharpness parameter with a range from 0 to 6.

contrast
An image parameter set to 100 in the CaptureSettings, influencing the difference between light and dark areas.<SEP>Contrast parameter with a range from 0 to 255.

cam.SetCaptureSettings
A method used to apply capture settings to the camera for specific function modes.

exposure settings
A photography parameter mentioned in the error message when settings fail to apply.

Ubuntu
An operating system platform where g++ is recommended for development.<SEP>Ubuntu is the operating system environment where the libMediaSDK is installed and tested, specifically version 18.04.<SEP>Ubuntu is a Linux distribution explicitly mentioned as a supported platform for MediaSDK-Cpp.

/doc directory
A documentation resource location containing additional information about the camera software.

stitcherSDKDemo.exe
A software tool used for stitching media files, supporting various input and output formats and parameters.<SEP>A standalone executable tool for media stitching and processing, part of the MediaSDK.

data.content:
MediaSDK
A software development kit (SDK) that includes the stitcherSDKDemo.exe tool for media processing.<SEP>A software development kit for media processing, including tools like stitcherSDKDemo.exe.<SEP>The core software development kit library linked during compilation of the demo application.

data.content:
5.7K素材
High-resolution media files (5.7K) that consist of two fisheye videos (00 and 10 files) for stitching.

非5.7K素材
Lower-resolution media files that contain two fisheye videos in a single file for stitching.

图片素材
Image files (insp or jpg format) that contain merged fisheye images for stitching.

视频输出
Output files in mp4 format, with options for resolution, bitrate, and encoding (H.264 or H.265).

图片输出
Output files in jpg or png format, with options for resolution and image sequence output.

insta360 studio
A software recommended for playing stitched panoramic media files.

potplayer
A media player recommended for viewing stitched panoramic media files.

template拼接
Basic stitching method with standard performance/quality tradeoffs.

dynamicstitch拼接
Advanced dynamic stitching method for improved quality at higher computational cost.

optflow拼接
Optical-flow based stitching method offering highest quality with maximum processing time.

H.264编码
Default video encoding standard for output files.

H.265编码
Alternative video encoding for 4K+ resolution support in CUDA environments.

CUDA
CUDA is a parallel computing platform and API model created by NVIDIA, with version 11.1 mentioned in the context.<SEP>NVIDIA's parallel computing platform affecting hardware acceleration options.<SEP>Parallel computing platform that can be disabled for processing.<SEP>CUDA is a parallel computing platform that is partially supported by MediaSDK-Cpp on Ubuntu and fully supported on Windows.

data.content:
PotPlayer
PotPlayer is a media player recommended for optimal viewing of panoramic content.<SEP>PotPlayer is a multimedia software application that supports viewing panoramic content with appropriate settings.
data.file_path: MediaSDK Demo User Guide.md<SEP>MediaSDK Demo User Guide zh.md

data.content:
Camera Accessory Type
A parameter setting related to the type of protective lens used in camera equipment.<SEP>The type of camera accessory is set using the '-camera_accessory_type' parameter.
data.file_path: MediaSDK Demo User Guide.md<SEP>MediaSDK Demo User Guide zh.md

data.content:
Enable Area Sample
A parameter used to eliminate moiré patterns when exporting low-resolution images.

Panoramic Content
Refers to 360-degree or wide-angle media content that requires specific software or settings for proper viewing.

Model File Path
Refers to the directory or location where model files are stored in the system.

Note
A general instruction or recommendation regarding panoramic content playback.

Insta360 Studio
A software application developed by Insta360 for viewing and editing panoramic content.

Low-Resolution Export
Refers to the process of exporting images or videos at reduced resolution settings.

Moiré Patterns
Undesirable visual artifacts that appear in certain patterns when exporting low-resolution images.

5.7K videos
High-resolution video files consisting of paired '.insv' files from fisheye lenses.

Non-5.7K videos
Lower-resolution video files combined into a single '.insv' file.

H265 (HEVC)
A video compression standard used for high-efficiency encoding, especially for resolutions beyond 4K.

H264
A widely used video compression standard, default for stitcherSDKDemo.exe.

template stitching
A method for combining images into a panorama, offering basic quality and faster processing.

dynamic stitching
An advanced method for panorama creation, offering higher quality but longer processing time.

optical flow stitching
The highest quality panorama stitching method, with the longest processing time.

flowstate
A stabilization feature to reduce motion-induced irregularities in footage.

directionlock
A feature to lock the viewing direction during playback, requiring flowstate stabilization.

stitch fusion
A feature to reduce chromatic aberrations in stitched panoramic images.

denoise
A noise reduction feature to minimize grain and noise in the final output.

colorplus
An enhanced color correction feature, optionally using a specified model file.

camera_accessory_type
A parameter to set the type of camera accessory used.

enable_area_sample
A feature to eliminate moiré when exporting to low resolution.

E:\workspace\MediaSDK\bin\MediaSDK20231212(windows)\MediaSDK\bin
The directory path where stitcherSDKDemo.exe is located and executed.

.insv files
Video file format used as input for stitching, distinguished by resolution (5.7K vs non-5.7K).

.insp files
Image file format containing dual fisheye projections for stitching.

.jpg files
Image file format supported as both input and output by the stitcher tool.

.mp4 files
Video output format supported by the stitcher tool.

.png files
Image sequence output format option.

1920x960 resolution
Default output resolution with 2:1 aspect ratio requirement.

moiré
Visual artifact that can be eliminated using area sampling.

chromatic aberrations
Color distortions reduced by stitch fusion feature.

Insta360's Studio
Insta360's Studio is a software recommended for optimal viewing of panoramic content.

Enhanced Color Correction
Enhanced color correction features are engaged using the '-enable_colorplus' option, with model file path specified via '-colorplus_model'.

Area Sample
Area sample is used to eliminate moiré when exporting to low resolution, enabled by the '-enable_area_sample' parameter.

Reduction
Reduction is a process used to minimize undesired grain and noise from the final output.

Enable_colorplus
Enable_colorplus is an option that engages enhanced color correction features, with model file path specified via '-colorplus_model'.

Colorplus_model
Colorplus_model is the parameter used to specify the model file path when enabling enhanced color correction features.

Camera_accessory_type
Camera_accessory_type is a parameter used to set the type of camera accessory.

Enable_area_sample
Enable_area_sample is a parameter used to eliminate moiré when exporting to low resolution.

Moiré
Moiré is an undesired visual artifact that can be eliminated using the '-enable_area_sample' parameter.

Panoramic content
Panoramic content refers to wide-angle or 360-degree media that requires specialized software for optimal viewing.

libMediaSDK-dev
libMediaSDK-dev is a software development kit (SDK) package used for media-related development, installable and removable via dpkg commands.

MediaSDKDemo
MediaSDKDemo is a demonstration application that verifies the successful installation of libMediaSDK and allows for debugging.<SEP>MediaSDKDemo is a demonstration tool used to verify the successful installation and functionality of the Media SDK.

cuda
cuda is a parallel computing platform and API model created by NVIDIA, with version 11.1 mentioned as a requirement or note.

libMediaSDK-dev_2.0-0_amd64_ubuntu18.04.deb
The specific .deb package file for installing libMediaSDK-dev on Ubuntu 18.04 systems.

dpkg
Debian package management system used to install and remove Media SDK.<SEP>The Debian package management system tool used to install, remove, and manage .deb packages.

main.cc
A C++ source code file in the example directory that can be modified for debugging purposes.<SEP>A C++ source file example that can be modified and compiled with Media SDK.

testSDKDemo
The output executable generated when compiling main.cc with the MediaSDK library.

C++11
The C++ language standard version specified for compiling the example code.

Ubuntu 18.04

The specific version of the GNU C++ compiler mentioned for building the software.

data.content:
Media SDK
The Media SDK is a software development kit used for media-related development, installation, and debugging.

example directory
Directory containing sample code files for Media SDK usage.

MediaSDK-Cpp
MediaSDK-Cpp is a C++ library designed for stitching and editing media from Insta360 360 cameras.

Video Stitcher
The Video Stitcher is a component of MediaSDK-Cpp that handles asynchronous video stitching.

Image Stitcher
The Image Stitcher is a component of MediaSDK-Cpp that handles synchronous image stitching.

insv
Input video file format supported by MediaSDK-Cpp

mp4
Output video file format supported by MediaSDK-Cpp

insp/jpeg
Input image file formats supported by MediaSDK-Cpp

jpg
Output image file format supported by MediaSDK-Cpp

FlowState
Image stabilization technology that can be enabled/disabled

DirectionLock
Camera orientation locking feature

StitchFusion
Advanced stitching algorithm feature

Denoise
Noise reduction feature

ColorPlus
Color enhancement feature

ImageStitcher
ImageStitcher is a software component used for stitching images, with configurable parameters like input path, stitch type, and output settings.

VideoStitcher
VideoStitcher is a software component for stitching videos, offering features like CUDA acceleration, color enhancement, and progress/error callbacks.

STITCH_TYPE
STITCH_TYPE is an enumeration defining different stitching algorithms: TEMPLATE (fast but less effective), OPTFLOW (slow but best quality), and DYNAMICSTITCH (balanced).

HDR_TYPE
HDR_TYPE is an enumeration defining HDR processing modes: None, SingleImageHdr, MultiImageHdr_mbb, and MultiImageHdr_mpl.

InputPaths
InputPaths define the source files for stitching, with specific rules for video (max 2 files) and image (multiple paths for HDR).

EnableFlowState
Boolean parameter to activate Insta360's proprietary super flowstate feature for enhanced stitching.

EnableDirectionLock
Boolean parameter to lock the viewing direction during video stitching.

EnableStitchFusion
Boolean parameter to enable image fusion during the stitching process.

EnableDenoise
Boolean parameter to activate noise reduction during video stitching.

EnableColorPlus
Boolean parameter to enable color enhancement with optional model path specification.

process_callback
Callback function that reports stitching progress percentage during video processing.

error_callback
Callback function that handles and reports errors during the stitching process.

Pro Camera API
Pro Camera API provides a way to execute commands asynchronously, particularly for time-consuming tasks like 'take_picture' and 'stop_record'.

take_picture
A command used in the Pro Camera API for capturing images, executed asynchronously with a sequence identifier for result retrieval.

stop_record
A command used in the Pro Camera API for stopping recordings, executed asynchronously with a sequence identifier for result retrieval.

get_result
A command in the Pro Camera API designed to retrieve the actual results of previously executed asynchronous commands using their sequence IDs.

State polling
A method introduced in the Pro Camera API to check the status of asynchronous tasks via the '_idRes' attribute in the state command response.

state
A command in Pro Camera API that provides system status including completed async operations through '_idRes' attribute.<SEP>The state indicates the completion status of the async command (e.g., 'done').<SEP>A field in response indicating whether the request was successfully executed (done/exception/error).

_idRes
An attribute in state command response that lists sequence IDs of completed asynchronous operations.<SEP>_idRes is an array of finished async command sequences in the state response.

sequence
A unique identifier returned by async commands that acts as a task token for result retrieval.<SEP>Sequence is an identifier used for handling asynchronous command responses.

camera._takePicture
The formal name of the take_picture command in Pro Camera API's response structure.<SEP>camera._takePicture is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to capture an image.

camera._getResult
The formal name of the get_result command in Pro Camera API's request/response structure.

res_array
The response array in get_result command containing results of requested async operations.

_picUrl
The result field containing image URL in take_picture command response.

Insta360 Pro
Insta360 Pro is a 360-degree camera with various resolution capabilities, supporting real-time stitching and 3D output.<SEP>Insta360 Pro is a camera model supported by the Insta360 Pro Camera API, featuring advanced control capabilities via HTTP requests.

Insta360 Pro2
Insta360 Pro2 is an advanced 360-degree camera with higher resolution, HDR support, and enhanced bitrate options.

Insta360 Titan
Insta360 Titan is a high-end 360-degree camera offering ultra-high resolutions and 3D output capabilities.<SEP>Insta360 Titan is a high-end camera model supported by the Insta360 Pro Camera API, designed for professional use.

Resolution
Resolution refers to the maximum output and single-lens capabilities of the Insta360 cameras, including support for 3D and real-time stitching.

Real-time Stitching
Real-time stitching is a feature in Insta360 cameras that allows for immediate panoramic or 3D output during recording.

3D Output
3D output is a capability of Insta360 cameras, enabling the creation of stereoscopic 360-degree content.

Single Lens Resolution
The resolution capability of individual camera lenses, ranging from 1920x1080 to 5280x3956 across different models.

Max Output Resolution
The maximum stitched output resolution available, varying by model from 3840x1920 to 10560x10560 for 3D content.

HDR Support
High Dynamic Range capability, exclusive to Insta360 Pro2 among these models.

Bitrate
The data rate of video recording, ranging from 1-40Mbps (Pro) to 1-180Mbps (Titan).

Frame Rate
The recording speed options from 30fps up to 120fps across different resolutions.

3840x2160@60fps
A video resolution and frame rate specification indicating high-definition video at 60 frames per second.

7680x3840
An ultra-high-resolution video format, likely for panoramic or VR applications.

2624x1486@120fps
A high-frame-rate video resolution suitable for slow-motion or high-action capture.

5248x2624
A high-resolution format, potentially for 360-degree or immersive video.

5248x5248(3D)
A stereoscopic 3D video format with square aspect ratio for depth perception.

1920x1080@240fps
Full HD resolution at extremely high frame rate for super slow-motion capture.

3840x1920
A wide-aspect high-resolution format, possibly for VR180 applications.

1~180Mbps
A variable bitrate range indicating high-quality video compression requirements.

get_image_param
A command that retrieves all image parameters at once for a camera.

aaa_mode
3A mode of the camera with values like Auto Mode, Manual Mode, WDR Mode, etc.

wb
White balance parameter with values representing different color temperatures.

iso_value
ISO sensitivity values ranging from 100 to 6400.

shutter_value
Shutter speed values ranging from 2 seconds to 1/8000s.

hue
Hue parameter with a range from -15 to 15.

ev_bias
Exposure bias parameter with a range from -255 to 255.

camera._getImageParam
The command name for retrieving all image parameters from a camera.<SEP>camera._getImageParam is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to obtain image parameters.

Auto Mode
A camera 3A mode where settings are automatically adjusted by the camera.

Manual Mode
A camera 3A mode where settings are manually controlled by the user.

WDR Mode
Wide Dynamic Range mode for handling high contrast scenes.

Shutter Priority Mode
A mode where shutter speed is prioritized and other settings adjust accordingly.

ISO Priority Mode
A mode where ISO sensitivity is prioritized and other settings adjust accordingly.

White Balance
Camera parameter controlling color temperature adjustments.

2700K
A warm white balance setting for artificial lighting conditions.

3200K
A white balance setting for tungsten lighting.

4000K
A white balance setting for fluorescent lighting.

5000K
A daylight white balance setting.

6500K
A cool white balance setting for overcast conditions.

7500K
A very cool white balance setting for shady conditions.

ISO
Camera sensitivity setting with values from 100 to 6400.

Shutter Speed
Camera parameter controlling exposure duration with values from 2s to 1/8000s.

Brightness
Image brightness adjustment parameter ranging from -255 to 255.

Contrast
Image contrast adjustment parameter ranging from 0 to 255.

Saturation
Color intensity adjustment parameter ranging from 0 to 255.

Hue
Color tone adjustment parameter ranging from -15 to 15.

Sharpness
Image detail enhancement parameter ranging from 0 to 6.

EV Bias
Exposure compensation parameter ranging from -255 to 255.

connect
The 'connect' command establishes a session between a camera and a client, enabling exclusive access to the camera for the duration of the session.

camera._connect
The 'camera._connect' event involves synchronizing time between the client and the camera, and returns detailed camera information upon successful connection.<SEP>camera._connect is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to establish a connection with the camera.<SEP>camera._connect is a command used to establish a connection with a camera, requiring parameters like hardware time and time zone.

pro2
The 'pro2' is a camera model mentioned in the response, indicating the type of camera being used.

titan
The 'titan' is another camera model mentioned as a possible value in the response.

Fingerprint
Fingerprint is a string value used for client identification, obtained by the `connect` command.<SEP>The 'Fingerprint' is a unique identifier for the session, used in subsequent commands to maintain the connection.

url_list
The 'url_list' contains URLs for live, preview, and record streams provided by the camera.

last_info
The 'last_info' section contains detailed camera information, including state, version, and current actions like preview, recording, and live streaming.

sys_info
The 'sys_info' provides basic camera information such as kernel version, ROM version, and serial number.

client
The client is the user or system that initiates the 'connect' command to establish a session with the camera.

camera
The camera is the device that establishes a session with the client and provides streaming and recording capabilities.<SEP>The camera is the device that hosts the storage and executes the listFiles command.

UTC Time
UTC time is used for synchronization between the client and the camera during the 'connect' command.

GTM Time Zone
GTM time zone string is part of the time synchronization parameters in the 'connect' command.

machine
The 'machine' attribute indicates the camera model (e.g., 'pro2' or 'titan') in the response.

origin
Configuration for unstitched fisheye video, including resolution, framerate, and bitrate.<SEP>Describes the capture options for each lens, including parameters like mime type, width, height, framerate, bitrate, and saveOrigin.

preview
Options for the stitched preview stream, including resolution, framerate, and bitrate.

live
Options for the stitched live stream, including URL, duration, and HDMI output status.

record
Options for the stitched recording, including URL, duration, and encoding settings.

audio
Audio configuration, including format, sample rate, and bitrate.<SEP>Describes the audio options, including mime type, sample format, channel layout, sample rate, and bitrate.

kernel version (k_v)
Part of 'sys_info', indicating the camera's kernel version.

ROM version (r_v)
Part of 'sys_info', indicating the camera's ROM version.

serial number (sn)
Part of 'sys_info', uniquely identifying the camera.

State Polling
State Polling is a feature of the Insta360 Pro Camera API that allows clients to monitor the camera's status.<SEP>State polling is a method used for maintaining a connection with the camera and receiving notifications.

_battery
_battery is a response field indicating battery level and charging status.

_external_dev
_external_dev contains information about file paths on the camera's main storage.

_cam_state
_cam_state is a camera state mask in the state response.

_gps_state
_gps_state indicates the GPS device status and location accuracy.

_tl_info
_tl_info provides timelapse information, including the count of pictures taken.

_snd_state
_snd_state contains microphone device information, including type and spatial capabilities.

_left_info
_left_info provides estimated storage information, including remaining recording time.

fan_level
fan_level indicates the fan level of the camera.

Client
The system or user that interacts with the camera by sending state commands and receiving responses.

State Command
A heartbeat mechanism sent to maintain connection, requiring no parameters except standard headers.

Battery Status
Includes battery level percentage and charging state in the camera's status response.

Async Commands
Completed command sequences tracked through the _idRes array in status responses.

External Storage
Manages file paths and default save locations on the camera's storage system.

Camera State
Bitmask representing various operational states of the camera hardware.

GPS Status
Indicates GPS device presence and location accuracy (none/2D/3D).

Timelapse Data
Tracks number of images captured during timelapse operations.

Microphone Info
Details about connected audio devices including type and spatial capabilities.

Storage Capacity
Estimates remaining recording time for different operation modes.

Cooling System
Fan level indicator for camera temperature management.

Insta360 Pro Camera API
The Insta360 Pro Camera API defines a set of commands to control Insta360 Pro series cameras, including models like Insta360 Pro, Insta360 Pro 2, and Insta360 Titan.

Insta360 Pro 2
Insta360 Pro 2 is a camera model supported by the Insta360 Pro Camera API, offering enhanced functionality over its predecessor.

Built-in Http Server
The built-in HTTP server in Insta360 Pro series cameras facilitates communication via the API, supporting commands, state polling, and file access.

JSON
JSON is a lightweight data-interchange format used for structuring data in requests and responses.<SEP>JSON is the data format used for both the request and response of the listFiles command.<SEP>JSON is the data format used for encoding commands and responses in the Insta360 Pro Camera API.<SEP>The data interchange format used for both the request parameters and response structure in the camera command API.

HTTP
HTTP is the protocol used for transferring commands and data between clients and Insta360 Pro cameras via the API.

RTMP
RTMP is the streaming protocol used for previewing live footage from Insta360 Pro cameras.<SEP>Real-Time Messaging Protocol, used for streaming audio, video, and data over the internet.<SEP>Real-Time Messaging Protocol used for live streaming video delivery.

Async Command Handling
Async Command Handling refers to the API's capability to manage asynchronous command execution.

Commands
Commands are the specific instructions that can be executed via the Insta360 Pro Camera API, such as taking pictures or starting recordings.

Capabilities
Capabilities refer to the features and functionalities supported by the Insta360 Pro Camera API.

camera._setOptions
A command that allows setting options for a camera, supporting both single and batch options.<SEP>camera._setOptions is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to configure camera settings.

camera._getOptions
The 'camera._getOptions' command is a specific function to retrieve camera options, with parameters and response details defined.<SEP>camera._getOptions is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to retrieve camera settings.

camera._startPreview
camera._startPreview is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to initiate a live preview.

camera._stopPreview
camera._stopPreview is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to terminate a live preview.

camera._startRecording
camera._startRecording is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to begin video recording.

camera._stopRecording
camera._stopRecording is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to end video recording.

camera._startLive
camera._startLive is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to start a live stream.

camera._stopLive
camera._stopLive is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to stop a live stream.

camera._listFiles
camera._listFiles is a command in the Insta360 Pro Camera API used to retrieve a list of files stored on the camera.<SEP>camera._listFiles is the specific command name used to list files on the camera.

listFiles
listFiles is a command that retrieves a file list from a camera's storage.

Async command handling
Async command handling refers to the process of managing asynchronous commands like camera._listFiles.

state command
The state command provides information about the storage path used by the listFiles command.

storage
The storage is the system component where files are kept and accessed via the listFiles command.

path
The path specifies the directory location within the storage where files are listed.

parameters
Parameters are the input fields required for executing the listFiles command.

async command
An async command is one that processes in the background and requires result handling.

get_options
The 'get_options' command is used to retrieve options from a camera, with the response format depending on the property specified.

property
A required parameter for the 'get_options' command, defining which camera option to retrieve.

value
The dynamic response data returned by 'camera._getOptions', whose structure depends on the queried property.

set_options
A command that allows configuring camera options, supporting both single and batch settings

Single Options
Configuration mode where individual camera properties are set one at a time

Batch Options
Configuration mode where multiple camera properties are set simultaneously

Property
A configurable parameter of the camera that can be modified through the set_options command

Value
The setting assigned to a camera property, which can be of various data types

Response
The output returned after executing the camera._setOptions command

State
Indicates whether the options setting operation completed successfully or encountered errors

Results
Contains detailed error information when batch options processing fails

HTTP POST
HTTP POST is a method used for sending data to a server to create or update a resource.

Content-Type
Content-Type is an HTTP header specifying the media type of the resource, commonly set to 'application/json'.

error
error is an object containing detailed information about a failed command execution, including error code and description.

hw_time
A parameter in camera._connect command specifying hardware time in MMDDhhmm[[CC]YY][.ss] format.

time_zone
A parameter in camera._connect command specifying the time zone in GMT format.

results
A field in successful responses containing data returned by the command.

code
A field in error objects containing the numerical error code.

description
A field in error objects containing textual error description.

start_preview
A command that instructs the camera to push an RTMP preview stream, involving parameters like origin, stiching, and audio.

stiching
Describes the output of on-board stitching, including mode, mime type, width, height, framerate, and bitrate. Note: The attribute name is a typo but kept for compatibility.

H.264
Video compression standard used for origin and stitched output.

H.265
Alternative video compression standard mentioned as possible origin format.

JPEG
Image format option for origin capture.

RAW
Unprocessed image format option for origin capture.

AAC
Audio codec specified for audio stream.

Stereo
Audio channel layout for the stream.

Pano
Mono 360° stitching mode for video output.

3D_top_left
Stereo 360° stitching mode with left eye on top.

3D_top_right
Stereo 360° stitching mode with right eye on top.

Stabilization
Image stabilization feature available for live streaming output.<SEP>Video processing feature that can be applied to preview.

Insta360 Pro series camera
The Insta360 Pro series camera is a professional-grade camera offering two live modes: 'normal' and 'origin live', with varying resolutions and stitching capabilities.

Normal Mode
Normal mode pushes a single stitched equirectangular/cube mapped stream with a maximum resolution of 3840x3840 due to on-board stitching limits.

Origin Live Mode
Origin live mode pushes multiple fisheye streams (one per lens) for out-camera stitching, supporting resolutions up to 8K or 11K for Titan, requiring client-side real-time stitching.

Stitching
Stitching is a process available only in normal mode, combining multiple fisheye streams into a single equirectangular/cube mapped output.

Titan
Titan is a variant of the Insta360 Pro series camera capable of supporting 11K resolution in origin live mode.

HDMI
Interface for live output display available in Normal mode stitching configuration.

Audio Configuration
Parameters for audio stream including mime type, sample format, and bitrate settings.

Auto Connect
Feature enabling automatic reconnection attempts for live streaming sessions.
